reviews.review_description:
I own Iron Wolf on Steam and even though this has worse graphics this game is way more immersive. With all the things you can interact with this game it’s amazing.

Wish there were more people to play coop with . I did have connection issues in the past but for all the work these developers did to make such an amazing WW2 game last thing I would fuss about it multiplayer stuff. 

It’s sad too many gamers complain about petty things and too much expectations for this game to be a hit. Like gamers need an update for hundreds years or they consider it abandoned and not complete .
Yo fellow  ace here and the devs may never read this and will we ever see engagement with the game and an update because we more features or anything at this point but yet it still is a good game ￼￼

The difficulty level is so incredibly high that it is nearly impossible to do single player. Your only chance is to find a decent group online and pray that they have microphones. I can't repair leaks, shoot torpedoes, and maneuver at the same time.

Fun seeing effects, in spite of usual graphics to make work. So... went along, learned routine, but the game app will make sure you miss targets.  Got so tired of even point blank, even waited on 3 measurements,  and it made sure all torpedoes miss. Noticed what is even a direct hit, computer will make sure misses. Right between two close ships, in front (had it turn and skim off hull). Oh, and then here come the planes that guarantee will get blown up; can't avoid.
No. ruined it. So tired of it, just deleted it (yeah, that's what you want to do after paying some money!)  So... wouldn't start it.

developer_response.body:
Hi Captain,

We’re sorry to hear that the experience became frustrating for you. We understand how disappointing it can feel when gameplay mechanics don’t behave the way you expect, especially after investing time into learning the game.

The game is designed to be challenging, but it should still feel fair and enjoyable. Feedback like yours is very valuable to us, as it helps us identify areas that may need balancing or further improvements.

Thank you for giving the game a chance and for sharing your thoughts with us.

Warm regards,
Pawel, CEO of Titan GameZ

reviews.review_description:
The game is fun and stressful at times, but it can get very boring. More uboats would be nice with more equipment and mechanics. I doubt the game has been left to die by the devs as it looks like they usually post an update for the game q1 of every year. I'm hoping this year's update won't be a disappointment. 

Overall, it's an okay game with fun things to do and a lot to learn, but it can get stale after a while.

Game is pretty good, me and my friends enjoyed it. I wish they had more updates getting pushed out and there were more versions of U-boats and such. One thing I do wish is the people who made this game make a ￼ww2 tank crew game for oculus since there is only pc ones and i think it would be very fun since i enjoyed a sub game. 

otherwise game is good but can get boring sometimes, but also can’t do much at sea when you main goal is to strike down liners.

reviews.review_title: UBOAT review and a much needed HOW TO
reviews.review_description:
It's a hidden gem! Is it perfect?  
No. 
The hatch to torpedo tube 2 may be open in your view, but other players in Multiplayer games may not see it that way. Gauges sometimes don’t have the same readings for different players, etc.
But what this game gets right is beyond compare. It lets you live out your dreams of being submariners in WWII. 
Albeit, for the losing side. 
Yes, it’s very German. And if blowing up allied boats doesn’t sit well with you, then, okay, I get it. 

However, if you’ve seen the movie “Das Boot” then you’re in for a treat. And if you haven’t, you should. It’s a masterpiece of tension and incredible filmmaking. References to the film are everywhere, and the layout of the boat is almost exact down to the cuts of meat hanging from the rafters.
The game tutorial is "kacken." (look it up.) It gives you a good general introduction to the game, but that’s it. There's no in-depth understanding of the ship, its systems, or how the game works, which is why you see many negative reviews. It’s unfortunate. The game is amazing in multiplayer, and also very fun in Freegame for a single player, but...
Only if you know how it works. 
I'm writting a full manual for the game and I'll put a link here for anyone who wants to dive deeper. This is a work in progress, and I'll add more notes as I make new discoveries.  Feel free to contact me for a Google docs link.
Very immersive￼ i felt like i was in a uboat and fight for nazi germany￼￼

For dews: Could you make the morse code machine and the enigma machine usefull in multiplayer plz.

For new players: I sugest first lerning how to operate the AA gun and the cannon after that how to lode the torpedoes. If you don't know or aren't 100% sure how to operate samething don't touch/tinker with it, just ask a fellow crew member or the captain how it works and what it thas.

An absolutely obsessed with this game every time I go on my VR I had straight to this game so I can see if there's any servers available to join if there is not any I create my own waiting for people to join

While the submarine is pretty cool the game play is very shallow. The campaign is very short and the 'world' is just the same exact thing over and over again. No matter where you go in the world you are going to see the exact same harbor when you get there with the exact same four boats to sink. There is literally no replay ability even before you finish the campaign as you just do the same thing over and over. 
Could have been good if they put in the effort to actually craft a world and a variety of scenarios. But they didn't. 
On top of that the graphics are very crude and it leaves what there is of the world feeling very flat. 
Don't waste your money.

developer_response.body:
Hey! Thanks for sharing your thoughts. We totally understand where you’re coming from — the single-player campaign focuses on a specific type of experience and pacing, which isn’t for everyone.

That said, you might want to give multiplayer a try! It offers a completely different kind of tension and depth — coordinating a U-boat crew with real players brings a lot of variety, unpredictability, and replayability. Every mission feels different when your crewmates are human.

We’re continuing to expand and improve the game, and community feedback like yours really helps us steer in the right direction. Thanks for giving UBOAT a shot! ⚓

All the best, Pawel Titan GameZ CEO

reviews.review_description: I really like it since its ww2 era kinda reminds me of. Down periscope

reviews.review_title: It used to work but not anymore
reviews.review_description:
I re-installed the game but when i try to play it, it stops working, crashes.

it used to work tho

Awsome vr game, but it lacks content... Very great game for you and your squad! I do think there should be diffrent uboats to command, and more missions. I wont complian about the graphics because its a vr game, of corse i shouldnt expect 4k graphics! Its an awsome experince for submarine lovers! (like me) I do think its a tad bit expencive though.

It's a very great game and fun to play, but it would be really cool if you could play in a team with more than 4 people and if in the radio room to get a mission you would have to write something in Morse code but otherwise it's a very cool game.

reviews.review_title: Solid base, just needs some work!
reviews.review_description:
The game is really neat! It’s definitely “silent hunter lite”. There’s a slight learning curve, but it’s easy to learn. 

Multiplayer is a lot of fun, though there needs to be much more moderation capabilities. 

As of now, anyone that joins, can do any role. The host, should be the captain. The captain should be in charge of things like choosing missions.

Also, roles should be assigned. It doesn’t make sense for everyone to run around and do every role. Most of the time people run straight to the targeting scope and then no one is loading tubes or navigating.

As captain, we should be able to assign stations to roles and only those roles can manipulate the stations. 

Captain should be the only one with access to fast travel.

Not bad at all overall. Just needs some tweaking and some more detail. Shoot if I could write on the map, I could chart out enemy movements and launch torpedoes from lower than periscope depth

reviews.review_title: Very fun to play but lacks updates
reviews.review_description: This game when I first bought it was very enjoyable to play but after a while it got very bland and boring because there was no updates but I started playing again because I wanted to try something else than any other game and the game is still fun but please update the game!
I'ts a very fun game I love the story and I'ts fun to play with friends
the physics are great but the big substitute distracts me and it's still there i want my refund

developer_response.body:
Hi Captain,

We’re really sorry to hear that something in the game distracted you enough to take away from the experience. That’s definitely not what we want for our players.

Thanks for pointing it out - feedback like this genuinely helps us improve. We’re continuously working on updates and polishing the game, and we hope you’ll consider giving it another try in the future.

We truly appreciate you giving the game a chance.

Warm regards,
Pawel, CEO of Titan GameZ

reviews.review_description:
I didn't play very long.  I got through the first 5 or 6 missions in single player and very quickly got sick and tired of sailing back to Port after every encounter to report to the captain.  This mechanic of returning to base after each mission is common in games with fighter planes, but it does not work in a submarine game.  WW2 submarines deployed for months at a time, not for one mission against a single target at a time.

Also, are we just going to pretend that we're not playing nazis?

As for the rest of the game play, it has some potential.  Things are a little slow and tedious, but that is part of submarine warfare.  My main complaint comes when spinning dials it does not track and follow the hand well, so you have to go slow and careful to make sure it follows along.  That and there is no way to set height and so everything is off.  In general everything and everyone seem to be at 80-90% scale and weirdly small.

developer_response.body:
Hi Captain,
Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts and describe your experience in detail. We appreciate hearing how different design choices and mechanics were perceived during your time with the game.

Your comments regarding mission structure, pacing, and interaction precision provide valuable context on how the overall flow and scale of the experience can feel to players. We also understand that historical themes and perspectives can influence how players connect with the setting, and it’s important for us to be aware of those reactions.

We’re glad to hear that you see potential in the core concept, and we sincerely appreciate you giving the game a try and sharing your honest impressions.

Warm regards,
Pawel, CEO of Titan GameZ

reviews.review_description: Was very fun. And there is always a chance for something funny to happen 5 stars
I will write a more in depth review here, but until then I absolutely love the detail and I hope the team keeps adding and improving. This is absolutely amazing!

This game is very immersive and very cooperative my friend and I have a lot of fun just shooting planes and sinking simple targets. However, the subs anatomy is difficult to navigate as well as memorizing all of the controls and functions by ourselves (my step dad who was former Navy had no issues) without playing any solo missions (which are at least 20 mins each) which is why in my personal opinion instead of teaching us bit by bit solo mission by solo mission add a standard tutorial mode. Maybe have us placed at a hands-on German STC training center.
|P.S keep up the good work my friends and I will continue to play and maybe I’ll get more of my friends on|

Why you say no crouch button. Bro there is a handle you can hold and that person that said play sail BRO WDYM GAME IS A$$
 YOU ARE JUST A$$ AT THE GAME BEST GAME EVER

I got this from meta quest plus and tried it today, and it blew me away, do detailed and realistic (not the graphics the gameplay) you go out on missions on a uboat, as far as i know. You sail it you load torpedoes, make it dive, pump water, pump air and shoot down ships. Probably a lot more to do. And its multiplayer? For only £15, thats a steal.

I like idea you went with, this is the first vr game where you can be in a submarine there are still bugs but this game is amazing. It would be awesome if you could add pvp or you could have more subs in one game and could communicate through radio or a phone

- Gman
Its a great concept but its done well but it could be better still great.

This is the first ever review I’ve been bothered to write in over half a decade of playing VR. Playing this masterpiece of a game after watching Das Boot for the first time hits DIFFERENT. If you surrender yourself to this, you too will find yourself randomly uttering lines from the film (in german no less). I can tell how much of a labour of love this was for the devs what with the painstaking details, modelling, features, and true to era accuracy. I’m eternally grateful for such an incredible experience. You have my undying loyalty & support as a fan.

On meta quest 3, the game autosaves during the first 4 missions. When I reach mission 5, after destroying the aircraft carrier, the game also autosaves. After that, the game stops autosaving automatically during gameplay and also fails to autosave when leaving the game to go to the menu. Without being able to save progress, the game becomes unplayable. I have over three gigabytes of space so not a lack of free space

developer_response.body:
Hi Captain,
Thank you for taking the time to report this - and I’m truly sorry that the autosave issue disrupted your experience. Problems like this can make the game unplayable, and that’s the last thing we ever want for our players.

We’re currently investigating a rare issue affecting autosaves on Meta Quest 3 after Mission 5, and your detailed description is extremely helpful in narrowing it down. 

We appreciate you giving UBOAT a chance, and we’re committed to resolving this as quickly as possible so you can safely continue your campaign. Thank you again for helping us improve the experience for everyone.

Warm regards,
Pawel, CEO of Titan GameZ

reviews.review_title: Game is very fun in multiplayer
reviews.review_description: Game is great, personally I’m only interested in the multiplayer and have had a great time with it. Highly recommend.

reviews.review_title: One of the most fun games I've ever play
reviews.review_description:
I've been playing this since the very day it came out and it's been fun all the way it's sad that they haven't been doing much updates but there are a few things that I would like to say to the devs I get them making a realistic U-Boat simulator is not easy by any means especially in VR but there are three major things that me and other people have agreed that we think should be added 

Number one u-boat customization: in the sense that you can change the loadouts that year you both has like instead of having the small little anti-aircraft gun on the back have the quad barrel Old 40 mm anti-aircraft guns that would be there on the U-Boat type 7 c/41 also adding different u-boats like the U-Boat type 2 different variants and that sort of thing 


More missions: I've had a lot of people agree with me saying and wishing that there were more missions and also fixing some things in missions like aircraft now aircraft had very good vision over you boats but especially if you're in the early war. They wouldn't be able to hone in on you and even when you're 50 meters below still somehow be able to hit you accurately with depth charges it's just not that realistic 


More u-boats: I've had many people agree with me that y'all should add the type 9 u-boat I cannot remember the nickname for it but it was almost double the size of the type 7 and it would be fun as hell to play in and play as


Last thing more people in multiplayer: not by much but at least 6 to 8 players otherwise you don't really have enough for someone to be doing everything because then you have people that are multitasking that don't need to be multitasking if you had eight players you could have someone running one section of the ship on their own which would be completely acceptable but with four people when you're trying to take down an entire convoy with two battleships while getting hit by aircraft from the aircraft carrier as well it's kind of hard 


So is what I have to say about this game is that it is one of the most fun games you will ever play it has a good community yeah there's a okay amount of kids but it's still fun as hell to play and a good game I've enjoyed this for years and I always end up coming back to it one time or another
Bobby review is right on. Plus it gives you instructions to do something but does not recognize when you have accomplished the task. So it just repeats nonstop.

developer_response.body:
Hi Captain,
Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts. We’re sorry to hear that the experience didn’t meet your expectations, and we truly appreciate you adding your perspective to the discussion.

Your comments about mission recognition, world variety, and overall depth of gameplay are valuable insights for us. Feedback like this helps us better understand how different players experience the game and which elements stand out as most important.

We know that not every aspect will resonate with everyone, but hearing these details gives us a clearer picture of where expectations may differ. Thank you again for giving UBOAT a try and for taking the time to let us know how the experience felt from your side.

Warm regards,
Pawel, CEO of Titan GameZ

reviews.review_description: I love this game and go to it quite often when I wear my VR. Lately I've been playing a lot of solo free roam and now have over 27 million tons sunk. On free roam pick an area, then go to the radio room and turn in the radio, tune it in and you'll get a morse code signal. Go to the map and you'll see where the ships are located. The only thing I don't like is if you take a while to set up to get to a convoy or a ship, the game resets the ship locations. Usually to the other side of the map. Sometimes you have to adjust where you are to grab controlls, a little glitchy there. Still a great game.

reviews.review_title: Solid game wish there were more like it
reviews.review_description: The game is great. I just wish there was more action. And if we didn’t have a full crew, give us AI crew. But other than that, it is a really well put together a game that I hope to see another, just like it. ￼

reviews.review_description: One man running a sub and expects me to do everything perfectly. Unfortunately there’s either no one playing or I have to invite friends aka force them to buy game. If the ai actually did things it would be miles better.

Thank you for giving it a try, and we hope you might return in the future as we continue to improve and expand the experience!

Without giving any more spoilers, I’ll just say that if you enjoy simulators, this one will be a trophy in your collection! ￼
Great game so far and amazing idea but if you lose thought the tutorial can be hard also the as gun are hard to hit on be otherwise amazing
Better with friends although It’s got a good single player system
Really fun gameplay but one issue the stick drift thank you for adding teleport movement it’s so nice to have

General its a cool, but basic, simulator for ww2 uboats.  Torpedo targeting is too easy. Missions are limited. Multiplayer doesn't seem to want to connect anymore.  Not many people playing multiplayer, and the few that do are often young kids acting silly. There's no apparent controls over managing a multiplayer game other than setting it as private.  This seems to just make the game a one man multiplayer game where you have to do everything yourself.  In normal mode it's still easy to complete the mission this way.
This game is very good submarine and the map is not the best but over all the game is very good you should get this game
I’m on the 4th mission and this game is cool on the 3s

So it was fun at first to play with my friend but it is really boring after like 10 minutes the devs kinda abandoned it so there's no updates that I have seen in my time of having the game

reviews.review_title: Can I get a refund...on my time!
reviews.review_description: I love shooters and exploration games,but this game sucked me in for hours. On solo I just cruised and played in the u boat,and multiplayer I laughed so hard,the guy smoking 20 cigarettes and threw pipes,we had a banana fight then I handed TNT to a new guy and he accidentally set it off...we all died,my kids thought something was wrong with how hard I was laughing. I love shooting at British with the artillery, This game is the bees knees,add a Luger on a shoulder holder and some dudes on the enemy ships in a the future update pls,that would be saucy,and also I wish I would insta die if the u boat dives and I'm topside,lol. This game is a super fun gem
They should add other u boats like type 9 or 9c or something that would make a huge improvement on ww2 war would be us driving destroyers or battleships for other countries too

This game is amazing. It is so immersive and realistic. You can walk around the sub, operate all the controls, light up a cigarette. It feels real and alive. It is one of the best games I have played on this platform. I only wish there were other games like this with full ships and planes. I hope the devs keep adding to this game because it is an absolute gem.

reviews.review_title: Great not the best game ever
reviews.review_description:
I think this is one of the first games were you can command a submarine. This game is WW2 based so already a good start but I find that we need to add more things into this game. 

Single player is kind of boring because there is really no campaign just missions. But multiplayer is very fun and also sometimes funny. If you find the right lobby people will just work with you and play kindly. Also some players go to the extreme and act like actual German Sailors. (Although this is based on WW2 German Navy, players will start doing salutes and stuff that are kind of inappropriate)

This game is fun for Co-Op type players and can be fun for those people. Other people think that we should have PVP for the next update. Like Sub vs sub type stuff. Also maybe a full on Naval battle with controllable planes and ships. Just a recommendation. Good job devs
Its a realy good game but culd use some more uboats

I like to play realistic games 
And this is the only game that has realistic lighting,make it where you can interact with the people and add different types of vessels
Also does anyone know why they arent updating the game are they working on a new game

reviews.review_title: Stunning but to complex and frustrating
reviews.review_description: I love submarines and I should love this, and the visuals are fantastic, fist 4 missions or so are good fun as you learn the controls. Don’t buy this to play single player though. It’s far too complicated and difficult to grab the various levers and wheels needed to operate the boat. You find you lose the grip and have to find them again. It’s just far too complex and fiddly for one person to operate by themselves. Add to this the frustrating voice over repeatedly shouting the same commands over and over again.  I think multiplayer is really what this game is designed for and it probably excels at that, for single player it needs to be simplified to be playable .

I enjoyed many hours playing solo. After finishing all missions I tried Free mode, where I could go through the same missions. I went off the script and went to enemy harbors when had extra torpedoes. It was getting a bit boring when I suddenly remembered the training about the radio and I got a new point on map where I experienced first bugs. After arrival there were sounds of ships everywhere in all directions but no shups seen until I ran out of fuel. Diesel smoke was still there outside even when running on electric.
It would be nice to have the sunset behind the western horizon instead of the east. Also the moon stuck motionless at the top and wrong motions of stars lost  a star down for me. I am still to experience a multiplayer mode. 
Well Done!
I would like more missions in the campaign because the game is great
Been a lot of fun dumping hours in this game but theres only so much you can do. You guys have tons of potential thanks for the enjoyable memories

reviews.review_title: New fear unlock more updated please
reviews.review_description:
I love this game. I finally got another quest, so I can play with my son together. Solo is great, but in co-ops, this game is amazing, just running in the heat of battle. This game made me unlock a new fear when diving all the way to 200m. The sound of the water  pressure is unreal. 
I do wish for more updates it's been a while. 
Things I would love to see are maybe more storyline for single-player mode. 
More unique mission to choose from 
Having enemy submarine to fight with 
But the thing I truly want is the ability to add an npc to help fill a position in multiplayer like in single player. 
This is a must-have game. I wish it the best hope it gets better.
The game is amazing, but it doesn't have any add ons . I love games with add ons and this isn't  one with add ons
This game is absolutely incredible. Got my brothers to get it and sailing the seas with the lads has never been cooler. Only thing missing is more content!! Please keep updating this game!!

Just to start off with, it might be good for you lot to know that the development team have basically ditched this game and have not been heard for a few months, so don't expect updates. If you're okay with putting that aside though, the amount of in-depth management and interactive stuff is phenomenal. Almost every part of the ship requires manual operation, from the ballast tanks to dive and surface, torpedoes and sonar to the engines. This is not a casual game, especially in multiplayer where four crewmates cramped in such a small space can get the better of you. If you do buy this game, 1. Buy it on sale, and 2. Take the time to go through the tutorial and learn every inch of the ship instead of immediately hopping into multiplayer, so you don't impede the attempts of the rest of the crew to keep order.

reviews.review_title: One of the best vr games
reviews.review_description: They should make a sequel where you play as the ships trying to hunt U-boat
I am a fan of world war 2 subs and this game is next level multiplayer is chaotic but amazing taking on the subs power and sinking enemy ships ha! Ha!
The devs have abandoned the game don't waste your money and play something else

This game is awesome. Has everything you’d want; dynamic controls, fun combat, all that. However, a couple months ago the devs stated they’d be taking a 2-week break, and then they never came back. All-in-all, it’s a fun game, but don’t buy it if you expect updates of any kind.

More updates please
I highly recommend getting. Worth the cost
I like the game when i Play in singleplayer. I can do what ever i want, choose different missions and take control over everything. However in multiplayer, we can’t agree. Anyways, 5 stars

Uboat: The Silent Wolf is so good because you can explore the Atlantic Ocean, and if you dare, the Pacific Ocean. Also it is based off of real time and actual conflicts that happened in WWII. But the fact that it’s realistic, ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️!!
